Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: la Gender: feminine Number: singular Forms: singular: la cuarta; plural: las cuartas English source: A quarter is 1/4 or 25% of something. He paid a quarter for the candy.

English: salary Pronunciation: el SUEL-do Spanish meaning: Dinero que gana una persona en su trabajo. Meaning in English: A salary is how much money a person makes at his or her job. Spanish example: Conseguí un trabajo nuevo con mejor sueldo. Example in English: He got a new job with a better salary. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: el Gender: masculine Number: singular Forms: singular: el sueldo; plural: los sueldos English source: A salary is how much money a person makes at his or her job. He got a new job with a better salary.

English: scholarship Pronunciation: la BE-ka Spanish meaning: Ayuda económica para estudiar. Meaning in English: A scholarship is money given so someone can go to school. Spanish example: Obtuve una beca para pagar la universidad. Example in English: I got a scholarship to help me pay for university. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: la Gender: feminine Number: singular Forms: singular: la beca; plural: las becas English source: A scholarship is money given so someone can go to school. I got a scholarship to help me pay for university.

English: temporary Pronunciation: tem-po-RAL Spanish meaning: Que dura poco tiempo. Meaning in English: If something is temporary, it exists for a short time. Spanish example: Este coche es temporal; pronto compraré uno nuevo. Example in English: This car is only temporary; I’ll get a new one soon. English source: If something is temporary, it exists for a short time. This car is only temporary; I’ll get a new one soon.

English: treasure Pronunciation: el te-SO-ro Spanish meaning: Conjunto de objetos valiosos, como oro o joyas. Meaning in English: A treasure is a collection of valuable things, especially jewels or gold. Spanish example: Se hicieron ricos al encontrar el tesoro enterrado. Example in English: They became very rich when they found the buried treasure. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: el Gender: masculine Number: singular Forms: singular: el tesoro; plural: los tesoros English source: A treasure is a collection of valuable things, especially jewels or gold. They became very rich when they found the buried treasure.

English: urge Pronunciation: in-STAR Spanish meaning: Animar con fuerza para que alguien haga algo. Meaning in English: To urge someone is to try very hard to get them to do something. Spanish example: Él insistió para que creyeran su historia. Example in English: He urged them to believe his story. Grammar: Part of speech: verb Forms: infinitive: instar; -ar pattern: yo -o; tú -as; él/ella -a; nosotros -amos; ellos -an; check irregular or stem-changing forms separately English source: To urge someone is to try very hard to get them to do something. He urged them to believe his story.

English: coach Pronunciation: el en-tre-na-DOR Spanish meaning: Persona que entrena equipos o atletas. Meaning in English: A coach is a person who teaches sports. Spanish example: Mi entrenador se entusiasma mucho durante los partidos. Example in English: My coach gets very excited during games. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: el Gender: masculine Number: singular Forms: singular: el entrenador; plural: los entrenadores English source: A coach is a person who teaches sports. My coach gets very excited during games.

English: control Pronunciation: kon-tro-LAR Spanish meaning: Hacer que algo haga lo que quieres. Meaning in English: To control something is to make it do what you want. Spanish example: Para controlar la televisión, pulsa los botones. Example in English: To control the TV, just push the buttons. Grammar: Part of speech: verb Forms: infinitive: controlar; -ar pattern: yo -o; tú -as; él/ella -a; nosotros -amos; ellos -an; check irregular or stem-changing forms separately English source: To control something is to make it do what you want. To control the TV, just push the buttons.

English: description Pronunciation: la des-kri-PSİON Spanish meaning: Texto que dice cómo es alguien o algo. Meaning in English: A description of someone or something says what they are like. Spanish example: Di una descripción del hombre con el sombrero y el arma a la policía. Example in English: I gave a description of the man with the gun and hat to the police. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: la Gender: feminine Number: singular Forms: singular: la descripción; plural: las descripciones English source: A description of someone or something says what they are like. I gave a description of the man with the gun and hat to the police.

English: direct Pronunciation: di-RE-kto Spanish meaning: Que va sin rodeos. Meaning in English: If something is direct, it goes straight between two places. Spanish example: El camino verde es una ruta directa a mi casa. Example in English: The green path is a direct route to my house. English source: If something is direct, it goes straight between two places. The green path is a direct route to my house.

English: exam Pronunciation: el e-XA-men Spanish meaning: Prueba para evaluar conocimientos. Meaning in English: An exam is a test. Spanish example: Hice preguntas de práctica para el examen de matemáticas. Example in English: I did some practice questions for the math exam on the board. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: el Gender: masculine Number: singular Forms: singular: el examen; plural: los examenes English source: An exam is a test. I did some practice questions for the math exam on the board.

English: example Pronunciation: el e-HEM-plo Spanish meaning: Algo típico o modelo de algo. Meaning in English: An example of something is a thing that is typical of it. Spanish example: La Coca-Cola es un ejemplo de refresco. Example in English: Cola is an example of a soft drink. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: el Gender: masculine Number: singular Forms: singular: el ejemplo; plural: los ejemplos English source: An example of something is a thing that is typical of it. Cola is an example of a soft drink.

English: limit Pronunciation: el Lİ-mi-te Spanish meaning: Cantidad máxima o mínima permitida. Meaning in English: A limit is the largest or smallest amount of something that you allow. Spanish example: Mi madre puso un límite al tiempo de móvil. Example in English: My mother put a limit on how much I could use the phone. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: el Gender: masculine Number: singular Forms: singular: el límite; plural: los límites English source: A limit is the largest or smallest amount of something that you allow. My mother put a limit on how much I could use the phone.

English: local Pronunciation: lo-KAL Spanish meaning: Propio de una zona cercana; del barrio. Meaning in English: If something is local, it is nearby. Spanish example: El mercado local de mi barrio vende toda la comida que necesitamos. Example in English: The local market in my neighborhood sells all the food we need. English source: If something is local, it is nearby. The local market in my neighborhood sells all the food we need.

English: magical Pronunciation: MA-hi-ko Spanish meaning: Que parece especial o extraordinario. Meaning in English: Magical describes a quality that makes someone or something special. Spanish example: Los fuegos artificiales hicieron que el cielo se viera mágico. Example in English: The fireworks made the night sky look so magical. English source: Magical describes a quality that makes someone or something special. The fireworks made the night sky look so magical.

English: mail Pronunciation: el KOR-reo Spanish meaning: Cartas y paquetes enviados a personas. Meaning in English: Mail is letters and other things sent to people. Spanish example: Recibo mucho correo porque tengo amigos en todo el mundo. Example in English: I get a lot of mail because I have friends all over the world. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: el Gender: masculine Number: singular Forms: singular: el correo; plural: los correos English source: Mail is letters and other things sent to people. I get a lot of mail because I have friends all over the world.

English: novel Pronunciation: la no-BE-la Spanish meaning: Libro que cuenta una historia. Meaning in English: A novel is a book that tells a story. Spanish example: Escribió una novela magnífica sobre la China antigua. Example in English: He wrote a great novel about ancient China. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: la Gender: feminine Number: singular Forms: singular: la novela; plural: las novelas English source: A novel is a book that tells a story. He wrote a great novel about ancient China.

English: outline Pronunciation: el es-KE-ma Spanish meaning: Borrador o plan de un texto. Meaning in English: An outline is the plan for a story or essay. Spanish example: Antes de escribir mi ensayo, hice un esquema. Example in English: Before I wrote my essay, I made an outline. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: el Gender: masculine Number: singular Forms: singular: el esquema; plural: los esquemas English source: An outline is the plan for a story or essay. Before I wrote my essay, I made an outline.

English: poet Pronunciation: el POE-ta Spanish meaning: Persona que escribe poemas. Meaning in English: A poet is a person who writes poems. Spanish example: William Shakespeare fue uno de los mejores poetas. Example in English: William Shakespeare was one of the greatest poets. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: el Gender: masculine Number: singular Forms: singular: el poeta; plural: los poetas English source: A poet is a person who writes poems. William Shakespeare was one of the greatest poets.

English: print Pronunciation: im-pri-MİR Spanish meaning: Poner algo sobre papel. Meaning in English: To print something is to put it onto paper. Spanish example: Asegúrate de imprimir tu nombre claramente. Example in English: Make sure that you print your name clearly. Grammar: Part of speech: verb Forms: infinitive: imprimir; -ir pattern: yo -o; tú -es; él/ella -e; nosotros -imos; ellos -en; check irregular or stem-changing forms separately English source: To print something is to put it onto paper. Make sure that you print your name clearly.

English: scene Pronunciation: la es-SE-na Spanish meaning: Parte de una película o de un libro. Meaning in English: A scene is one part of a book or movie. Spanish example: Una película se compone de muchas escenas. Example in English: A movie is made up of many short pieces or scenes. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: la Gender: feminine Number: singular Forms: singular: la escena; plural: las escenas English source: A scene is one part of a book or movie. A movie is made up of many short pieces or scenes.

English: sheet Pronunciation: la O-ha Spanish meaning: Papel delgado y plano. Meaning in English: A sheet is a thin flat piece of paper. Spanish example: Solo necesité una hoja para hacer mi tarea. Example in English: I only needed a single sheet of paper to do my homework. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: la Gender: feminine Number: singular Forms: singular: la hoja; plural: las hojas English source: A sheet is a thin flat piece of paper. I only needed a single sheet of paper to do my homework.

English: silly Pronunciation: TON-to Spanish meaning: Que carece de seriedad. Meaning in English: People who are silly show a lack of serious thought. Spanish example: Cometí un error tonto al dejar caer el jarrón de mamá. Example in English: I made a silly mistake of dropping mom’s vase. English source: People who are silly show a lack of serious thought. I made a silly mistake of dropping mom’s vase.

English: store Pronunciation: la TİEN-da Spanish meaning: Lugar donde se compran cosas. Meaning in English: A store is a place where you can buy things. Spanish example: Compré algunas cosas en la tienda de comestibles. Example in English: I picked up a few things at the grocery store. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: la Gender: feminine Number: singular Forms: singular: la tienda; plural: las tiendas English source: A store is a place where you can buy things. I picked up a few things at the grocery store.

English: suffer Pronunciation: su-FRİR Spanish meaning: Sentir dolor o pasar por dificultades. Meaning in English: To suffer is to feel pain. Spanish example: El dolor de cabeza le hizo sufrir todo el día. Example in English: Her headache made her suffer all day. Grammar: Part of speech: verb Forms: infinitive: sufrir; -ir pattern: yo -o; tú -es; él/ella -e; nosotros -imos; ellos -en; check irregular or stem-changing forms separately English source: To suffer is to feel pain. Her headache made her suffer all day.

English: technology Pronunciation: la te-kno-lo-HİA Spanish meaning: Nuevos avances creados con ciencia. Meaning in English: Technology is new things made by using science. Spanish example: Le encanta la tecnología, como los ordenadores portátiles. Example in English: He loves technology such as laptop computers. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: la Gender: feminine Number: singular Forms: singular: la tecnología; plural: las tecnologías English source: Technology is new things made by using science. He loves technology such as laptop computers.

English: across Pronunciation: kru-SAR Spanish meaning: Ir desde un lado al otro de algo. Meaning in English: To go across something is to go to the other side of it. Spanish example: Caminó a través del tablero hasta el otro lado. Example in English: He walked across the board to the other side. Grammar: Part of speech: verb Forms: infinitive: cruzar; -ar pattern: yo -o; tú -as; él/ella -a; nosotros -amos; ellos -an; check irregular or stem-changing forms separately English source: To go across something is to go to the other side of it. He walked across the board to the other side.

English: breathe Pronunciation: res-pi-RAR Spanish meaning: Hacer que el aire entre y salga del cuerpo. Meaning in English: To breathe means to let air go in and out of your body. Spanish example: Necesitamos pulmones sanos para respirar bien. Example in English: We need strong healthy lungs to help us breathe well. Grammar: Part of speech: verb Forms: infinitive: respirar; -ar pattern: yo -o; tú -as; él/ella -a; nosotros -amos; ellos -an; check irregular or stem-changing forms separately English source: To breathe means to let air go in and out of your body. We need strong healthy lungs to help us breathe well.

English: characteristic Pronunciation: la ka-ra-kte-RİS-ti-ka Spanish meaning: Cualidad que muestra cómo es algo o alguien. Meaning in English: A characteristic is something that shows what a person or a thing is like. Spanish example: Una característica de los tigres es tener rayas negras. Example in English: One characteristic of tigers is their black stripes. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: la Gender: feminine Number: singular Forms: singular: la característica; plural: las características English source: A characteristic is something that shows what a person or a thing is like. One characteristic of tigers is their black stripes.

English: consume Pronunciation: kon-su-MİR Spanish meaning: Comer o beber algo. Meaning in English: To consume something means to eat or drink it. Spanish example: Jack consumió un plato entero de espagueti. Example in English: Jack consumed a whole plate of spaghetti. Grammar: Part of speech: verb Forms: infinitive: consumir; -ir pattern: yo -o; tú -es; él/ella -e; nosotros -imos; ellos -en; check irregular or stem-changing forms separately English source: To consume something means to eat or drink it. Jack consumed a whole plate of spaghetti.

English: excite Pronunciation: e-mo-sio-NAR Spanish meaning: Hacer que alguien se sienta feliz e interesado. Meaning in English: To excite someone means to make them happy and interested. Spanish example: Cuando supe del baile de la escuela, me emocioné. Example in English: I heard about the school dance on Friday. This excited me. Grammar: Part of speech: verb Forms: infinitive: emocionar; -ar pattern: yo -o; tú -as; él/ella -a; nosotros -amos; ellos -an; check irregular or stem-changing forms separately English source: To excite someone means to make them happy and interested. I heard about the school dance on Friday. This excited me.

English: extremely Pronunciation: e-xtre-ma-da-MEN-te Spanish meaning: De forma muy intensa o exagerada. Meaning in English: If something is extremely good, it is very, very good. Spanish example: Estaba extremadamente preocupado por él. Example in English: I was extremely worried about him. English source: If something is extremely good, it is very, very good. I was extremely worried about him.

English: fear Pronunciation: el MİE-do Spanish meaning: Sentimiento de temor. Meaning in English: Fear is the feeling of being afraid. Spanish example: Tengo mucho miedo al monopatín. Example in English: I have a great fear of skateboarding. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: el Gender: masculine Number: singular Forms: singular: el miedo; plural: los miedos English source: Fear is the feeling of being afraid. I have a great fear of skateboarding.

English: fortunate Pronunciation: a-for-tu-NA-do Spanish meaning: Que tiene suerte. Meaning in English: If you are fortunate, you are lucky. Spanish example: Tuve la suerte de conseguir un asiento. Example in English: I was fortunate to get a seat. English source: If you are fortunate, you are lucky. I was fortunate to get a seat.

English: happen Pronunciation: su-se-DER Spanish meaning: Ocurrir, a veces por casualidad. Meaning in English: If someone happens to do something, he or she does it by chance. Spanish example: Me pasé encontrando hoy a nuevos amigos en la escuela. Example in English: I happened to meet some new friends at school today. Grammar: Part of speech: verb Forms: infinitive: suceder; -er pattern: yo -o; tú -es; él/ella -e; nosotros -emos; ellos -en; check irregular or stem-changing forms separately English source: If someone happens to do something, he or she does it by chance. I happened to meet some new friends at school today.

English: length Pronunciation: la lon-hi-TUD Spanish meaning: Qué tan largo es algo de un extremo al otro. Meaning in English: The length of something is how long it is from one end to the other. Spanish example: La longitud del suelo es de tres metros. Example in English: The length of the floor is three meters. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: la Gender: feminine Number: singular Forms: singular: la longitud; plural: las longitudes English source: The length of something is how long it is from one end to the other. The length of the floor is three meters.

English: mistake Pronunciation: el er-ROR Spanish meaning: Cosa que se hace mal. Meaning in English: A mistake is something you do wrong. Spanish example: Mi jefe siempre me regaña cuando cometo un error. Example in English: My boss always yells at me when I make a mistake. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: el Gender: masculine Number: singular Forms: singular: el error; plural: los errores English source: A mistake is something you do wrong. My boss always yells at me when I make a mistake.

English: observe Pronunciation: o-bser-BAR Spanish meaning: Mirar algo con atención. Meaning in English: To observe something is to watch it. Spanish example: Brian observó la salida del sol sobre las montañas. Example in English: Brian observed the sun rising over the mountains. Grammar: Part of speech: verb Forms: infinitive: observar; -ar pattern: yo -o; tú -as; él/ella -a; nosotros -amos; ellos -an; check irregular or stem-changing forms separately English source: To observe something is to watch it. Brian observed the sun rising over the mountains.

English: opportunity Pronunciation: la o-por-tu-ni-DAD Spanish meaning: Posibilidad de hacer algo. Meaning in English: An opportunity is a chance to do something. Spanish example: Tuve la oportunidad de tomar fotos en la selva. Example in English: I had an opportunity to take pictures in the jungle. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: la Gender: feminine Number: singular Forms: singular: la oportunidad; plural: las oportunidades English source: An opportunity is a chance to do something. I had an opportunity to take pictures in the jungle.

English: prize Pronunciation: el PRE-mio Spanish meaning: Objeto o dinero que se da al ganador. Meaning in English: A prize is something of value that is given to the winner. Spanish example: Gané un premio por sacar la mejor nota en ciencias. Example in English: I won a prize for getting the best grade on my science test. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: el Gender: masculine Number: singular Forms: singular: el premio; plural: los premios English source: A prize is something of value that is given to the winner. I won a prize for getting the best grade on my science test.

English: race Pronunciation: la kar-RE-ra Spanish meaning: Competencia para ver quién es más rápido. Meaning in English: A race is a contest to see who is the fastest. Spanish example: Paul es un corredor rápido y ganó la carrera. Example in English: Paul is a fast runner, so he easily won the race. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: la Gender: feminine Number: singular Forms: singular: la carrera; plural: las carreras English source: A race is a contest to see who is the fastest. Paul is a fast runner, so he easily won the race.

English: realize Pronunciation: DAR-se KUEN-ta Spanish meaning: Entender de repente la verdad de algo. Meaning in English: To realize is to suddenly understand. Spanish example: Cuando salí de casa, me di cuenta de que había dejado la luz encendida. Example in English: After I left my house, I realized that I left the light on. English source: To realize is to suddenly understand. After I left my house, I realized that I left the light on.

English: respond Pronunciation: res-pon-DER Spanish meaning: Dar una respuesta a lo que alguien dijo. Meaning in English: To respond is to give an answer to what someone else said. Spanish example: Cuando el profesor hizo la pregunta, respondimos todos. Example in English: When the teacher asked the question, we all responded. Grammar: Part of speech: verb Forms: infinitive: responder; -er pattern: yo -o; tú -es; él/ella -e; nosotros -emos; ellos -en; check irregular or stem-changing forms separately English source: To respond is to give an answer to what someone else said. When the teacher asked the question, we all responded.

English: risk Pronunciation: el RİES-go Spanish meaning: Posibilidad de que ocurra algo malo. Meaning in English: A risk is a chance of something bad happening. Spanish example: Asumí un riesgo y subí la montaña nevada. Example in English: I took a risk and climbed the snowy mountain. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: el Gender: masculine Number: singular Forms: singular: el riesgo; plural: los riesgos English source: A risk is a chance of something bad happening. I took a risk and climbed the snowy mountain.

English: wonder Pronunciation: pre-gun-TAR-se Spanish meaning: Sentir curiosidad o querer saber algo. Meaning in English: To wonder is to ask yourself questions or have a need to know. Spanish example: La madre se preguntaba si tendría un niño o una niña. Example in English: The young mother wondered if she’d have a boy or a girl. English source: To wonder is to ask yourself questions or have a need to know. The young mother wondered if she’d have a boy or a girl.

English: yet Pronunciation: to-da-BİA Spanish meaning: Todavía no ha ocurrido hasta el momento presente. Meaning in English: Yet is used to say something has not happened up to now. Spanish example: No podemos salir todavía; todavía estamos comiendo. Example in English: We can’t go out yet; we’re still eating. English source: Yet is used to say something has not happened up to now. We can’t go out yet; we’re still eating.

English: art Pronunciation: el AR-te Spanish meaning: Actividad creativa que expresa ideas y emociones. Meaning in English: Art is something creative that expresses ideas and feelings. Spanish example: A mi hermana le gusta ver el arte del museo. Example in English: My sister likes to look at the art in the museum. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: el Gender: masculine Number: singular Forms: singular: el arte; plural: los artes English source: Art is something creative that expresses ideas and feelings. My sister likes to look at the art in the museum.

English: book Pronunciation: el Lİ-bro Spanish meaning: Conjunto de hojas impresas encuadernadas. Meaning in English: A book is a set of printed sheets of paper that are held together inside a cover. Spanish example: El hombre sonríe mientras lee su libro favorito. Example in English: The man smiles as he reads his favorite book. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: el Gender: masculine Number: singular Forms: singular: el libro; plural: los libros English source: A book is a set of printed sheets of paper that are held together inside a cover. The man smiles as he reads his favorite book.

English: clothes Pronunciation: la RO-pa Spanish meaning: Lo que las personas usan para vestirse. Meaning in English: Clothes are what people wear to cover their bodies. Spanish example: Compré ropa abrigada para el invierno. Example in English: I bought warm clothes for the cold winter. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: la Gender: feminine Number: singular Forms: singular: la ropa; plural: las ropas English source: Clothes are what people wear to cover their bodies. I bought warm clothes for the cold winter.

English: community Pronunciation: la ko-mu-ni-DAD Spanish meaning: Grupo de personas con un entorno o intereses comunes. Meaning in English: A community is a group of people who share the same area or interests. Spanish example: La comunidad se reunió para plantar árboles. Example in English: The community got together to plant trees. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: la Gender: feminine Number: singular Forms: singular: la comunidad; plural: las comunidades English source: A community is a group of people who share the same area or interests. The community got together to plant trees.

English: December Pronunciation: di-SİEM-bre Spanish meaning: Duodécimo mes del año. Meaning in English: December is the twelfth month of the year. Spanish example: El último día del año es el 31 de diciembre. Example in English: The last day of the year is December 31st. English source: December is the twelfth month of the year. The last day of the year is December 31st.

English: dinner Pronunciation: la SE-na Spanish meaning: Comida principal que se hace por la noche. Meaning in English: Dinner is the main meal eaten in the evening. Spanish example: Cené una hamburguesa. Example in English: I had a hamburger for dinner. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: la Gender: feminine Number: singular Forms: singular: la cena; plural: las cenas English source: Dinner is the main meal eaten in the evening. I had a hamburger for dinner.

English: end Pronunciation: ter-mi-NAR Spanish meaning: Detener o finalizar algo. Meaning in English: To end is to stop or finish. Spanish example: Están contentos porque el profesor quiso terminar la clase temprano. Example in English: They are happy because the teacher wanted to end the class early. Grammar: Part of speech: verb Forms: infinitive: terminar; -ar pattern: yo -o; tú -as; él/ella -a; nosotros -amos; ellos -an; check irregular or stem-changing forms separately English source: To end is to stop or finish. They are happy because the teacher wanted to end the class early.

English: exchange Pronunciation: in-ter-kam-BİAR Spanish meaning: Dar una cosa a cambio de otra. Meaning in English: To exchange is to give one thing in return for another. Spanish example: Los hombres intercambiaron tarjetas de visita. Example in English: The men exchange business cards. Grammar: Part of speech: verb Forms: infinitive: intercambiar; -ar pattern: yo -o; tú -as; él/ella -a; nosotros -amos; ellos -an; check irregular or stem-changing forms separately English source: To exchange is to give one thing in return for another. The men exchange business cards.

English: family Pronunciation: la fa-Mİ-lia Spanish meaning: Grupo de personas unidas por parentesco. Meaning in English: A family is a group of people who are related to each other. Spanish example: Hay cuatro personas en mi familia. Example in English: There are four people in my family. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: la Gender: feminine Number: singular Forms: singular: la familia; plural: las familias English source: A family is a group of people who are related to each other. There are four people in my family.

English: from Pronunciation: DE Spanish meaning: Indica un punto de origen o inicio. Meaning in English: From shows a starting place or position. Spanish example: Chris estudia de las 10 a las 12 cada mañana. Example in English: Chris studies from 10 o’clock to noon every morning. English source: From shows a starting place or position. Chris studies from 10 o’clock to noon every morning.

English: green Pronunciation: BER-de Spanish meaning: Color de la hierba o las hojas. Meaning in English: Green is the color of growing grass or leaves. Spanish example: No me gustan las manzanas verdes. Example in English: I don’t like green apples. English source: Green is the color of growing grass or leaves. I don’t like green apples.

English: home Pronunciation: el o-GAR Spanish meaning: Lugar donde vive una persona. Meaning in English: A person’s home is the place where that person lives. Spanish example: La familia gusta de estar en casa los fines de semana. Example in English: The family likes to stay at home on the weekends. Grammar: Part of speech: noun Article: el Gender: masculine Number: singular Forms: singular: el hogar; plural: los hogares English source: A person’s home is the place where that person lives. The family likes to stay at home on the weekends.

Meaning in English: The afterlife is a life that some people believe begins when a person dies. Spanish example: Creo que existe una vida después de la muerte. Example in English: I believe that there is an afterlife. English source: The afterlife is a life that some people believe begins when a person dies. I believe that there is an afterlife.

English: archaeology Pronunciation: ar-keo-lo-HİA Spanish meaning: La arqueología estudia los restos de sociedades antiguas. Meaning in English: Archaeology is the study of the remains left by ancient societies. Spanish example: Disfrutaba visitar las pirámides de Egipto porque ama la arqueología. Example in English: He enjoyed visiting the Great Pyramids of Egypt because he loves archaeology. English source: Archaeology is the study of the remains left by ancient societies. He enjoyed visiting the Great Pyramids of Egypt because he loves archaeology.

English: chamber Pronunciation: SA-la Spanish meaning: Una sala es un espacio cerrado usado para un propósito especial. Meaning in English: A chamber is a closed space or room used for a special purpose. Spanish example: La reunión se hizo en la sala de la facultad. Example in English: The meeting was held in the faculty chamber. English source: A chamber is a closed space or room used for a special purpose. The meeting was held in the faculty chamber.

English: channel Pronunciation: ka-NAL Spanish meaning: Un canal es un espacio alargado entre dos márgenes. Meaning in English: A channel is a long, deep space between two edges. Spanish example: El río abrió un canal entre las rocas. Example in English: The river cut a channel through the rocks. English source: A channel is a long, deep space between two edges. The river cut a channel through the rocks.

English: core Pronunciation: NU-kleo Spanish meaning: El núcleo es la parte central y principal de algo. Meaning in English: A core is the main or central part of something. Spanish example: La Tierra tiene un núcleo sólido interno. Example in English: Earth has a solid inner core. English source: A core is the main or central part of something. Earth has a solid inner core.

English: corridor Pronunciation: pa-SİL-lo Spanish meaning: Un pasillo es un espacio estrecho que conduce a otras áreas. Meaning in English: A corridor is a narrow passage that leads into other areas. Spanish example: Tomó el pasillo de la izquierda para ir a su oficina. Example in English: He took the corridor on the left to go to his office. English source: A corridor is a narrow passage that leads into other areas. He took the corridor on the left to go to his office.

English: distinct Pronunciation: dis-TİN-to Spanish meaning: Algo distinto es diferente o se nota claramente. Meaning in English: A distinct thing is different or stands out. Spanish example: Tiene un acento muy distintivo. Example in English: He has a distinct accent. English source: A distinct thing is different or stands out. He has a distinct accent.

English: elite Pronunciation: E-li-te Spanish meaning: La élite es un grupo de alto nivel. Meaning in English: Elite means of or from a high-level group. Spanish example: Sólo un grupo élite podía entrar al club. Example in English: Only an elite group was allowed membership into the club. English source: Elite means of or from a high-level group. Only an elite group was allowed membership into the club.

English: engineer Pronunciation: in-he-NİAR Spanish meaning: Ingeniar algo es planear hábilmente cómo construirlo. Meaning in English: To engineer something is to skillfully plan out how to make that thing. Spanish example: Ingeniaron el robot y luego buscaron las piezas correctas. Example in English: After engineering the robot, they needed to find the correct parts to build it. After engineering the robot, they needed to find the correct parts to build it.

English: found Pronunciation: fun-DAR Spanish meaning: Fundar algo es iniciar o establecerlo. Meaning in English: To found something means to start, organize, or establish that thing. Spanish example: Mi abuelo fundó City Bank. Example in English: My grandfather founded the City Bank. Grammar: Part of speech: verb Forms: infinitive: fundar; -ar pattern: yo -o; tú -as; él/ella -a; nosotros -amos; ellos -an; check irregular or stem-changing forms separately English source: To found something means to start, organize, or establish that thing. My grandfather founded the City Bank.

English: gap Pronunciation: BRE-ça Spanish meaning: Una brecha es el espacio entre dos cosas. Meaning in English: A gap is a space between two things. Spanish example: Hay una pequeña brecha entre los bloques de madera. Example in English: There is a small gap between the blocks of wood. English source: A gap is a space between two things. There is a small gap between the blocks of wood.

English: glory Pronunciation: GLO-ria Spanish meaning: La gloria es grandeza o importancia especial. Meaning in English: Glory is the importance, magnificence, or specialness of something. Spanish example: Disfrutaron de la gloria del hermoso atardecer. Example in English: They enjoyed the glory of the beautiful sunset. English source: Glory is the importance, magnificence, or specialness of something. They enjoyed the glory of the beautiful sunset.

English: interior Pronunciation: in-te-RİOR Spanish meaning: El interior es la parte de adentro de algo. Meaning in English: An interior is the inside of something. Spanish example: Miraron el interior de la caja. Example in English: They looked at the interior of the box. English source: An interior is the inside of something. They looked at the interior of the box.

English: lion Pronunciation: LEON Spanish meaning: El león es un gran animal de la familia de los felinos. Meaning in English: A lion is a large animal in the cat family. Spanish example: Fuimos a ver al león en el zoológico. Example in English: We went to see the lion at the zoo. English source: A lion is a large animal in the cat family. We went to see the lion at the zoo.

English: role Pronunciation: ROL Spanish meaning: Un rol es un puesto o parte en algo. Meaning in English: A role is a job, position, or part in something. Spanish example: Su rol en la oficina es vender productos a clientes. Example in English: Her role in the office is to sell products to customers. English source: A role is a job, position, or part in something. Her role in the office is to sell products to customers.

English: royal Pronunciation: REAL Spanish meaning: Real significa relacionado con un rey o una reina. Meaning in English: Royal means related to a king or queen. Spanish example: La duquesa formaba parte de la familia real. Example in English: The Duchess was part of the royal family. English source: Royal means related to a king or queen. The Duchess was part of the royal family.

English: sole Pronunciation: U-ni-ko Spanish meaning: Solo o único significa el único. Meaning in English: A sole person or thing is the only one. Spanish example: Ella fue la única mujer de la habitación. Example in English: She was the sole woman in the room. English source: A sole person or thing is the only one. She was the sole woman in the room.

English: stairs Pronunciation: es-ka-LE-ras Spanish meaning: Las escaleras son una serie de escalones para pasar de un nivel a otro. Meaning in English: Stairs are a set of steps built to go from one level of a building to another. Spanish example: Tomó las escaleras en lugar del ascensor. Example in English: He took the stairs instead of the elevator. English source: Stairs are a set of steps built to go from one level of a building to another. He took the stairs instead of the elevator.

English: surface Pronunciation: su-per-Fİ-sie Spanish meaning: La superficie es la capa superior de algo. Meaning in English: The surface is the top layer of something. Spanish example: Limpié la superficie de la mesa. Example in English: She wiped the surface of the table. English source: The surface is the top layer of something. She wiped the surface of the table.

English: agreement Pronunciation: a-KUER-do Spanish meaning: Un acuerdo es una decisión formal sobre una acción futura. Meaning in English: An agreement is a formal decision about future action. Spanish example: Creo que obtendrás el acuerdo de Tom para está propuesta. Example in English: I think you’ll get Tom’s agreement to this proposal. English source: An agreement is a formal decision about future action. I think you’ll get Tom’s agreement to this proposal.

English: arise Pronunciation: sur-HİR Spanish meaning: Surgir significa que algo sucede. Meaning in English: To arise is to happen. Spanish example: Surgieron dificultades con su computadora vieja. English: benefactor Pronunciation: be-ne-fa-KTOR Spanish meaning: Un benefactor es quien da dinero para ayudar a alguien. Meaning in English: A benefactor is a person who gives money to help someone. Spanish example: El benefactor del estudiante le dio dinero para estudiar. Example in English: The student’s benefactor gave him money to spend on his studies. English source: A benefactor is a person who gives money to help someone. The student’s benefactor gave him money to spend on his studies.

English: blacksmith Pronunciation: er-RE-ro Spanish meaning: Un herrero es una persona que trabaja el metal. Meaning in English: A blacksmith is a person who makes things out of metal. Spanish example: El herrero martilleó el metal hasta dejarlo plano. Example in English: The blacksmith pounded the piece of metal until it was flat. English source: A blacksmith is a person who makes things out of metal. The blacksmith pounded the piece of metal until it was flat.

English: chimney Pronunciation: çi-ME-nea Spanish meaning: La chimenea es una pipa alta para sacar humo de un edificio. Meaning in English: A chimney is a tall pipe used to carry smoke out of a building. Spanish example: El gato se sentó en el tejado junto a la chimenea. The cat was sitting on the roof next to the chimney.

English: compensate Pronunciation: kom-pen-SAR Spanish meaning: Compensar es pagar a alguien por el tiempo dedicado. English: forge Pronunciation: for-HAR Spanish meaning: Forjar es fabricar o crear, a veces con dificultad. Meaning in English: To forge is to make or produce, especially with difficulty. Spanish example: Stacy y Heather forjaron su amistad cuando eran adolescentes. Example in English: Stacy and Heather forged their friendship when they were teenagers. Grammar: Part of speech: verb Forms: infinitive: forjar; -ar pattern: yo -o; tú -as; él/ella -a; nosotros -amos; ellos -an; check irregular or stem-changing forms separately English source: To forge is to make or produce, especially with difficulty. English: humble Pronunciation: u-MİL-de Spanish meaning: Una persona humilde no se cree mejor que los demás. Meaning in English: People who are humble do not believe that they are better than other people. Spanish example: Aunque Bob sea el más inteligente, es humilde. Example in English: Even though Bob is the smartest boy in his class, he is humble. English source: People who are humble do not believe that they are better than other people. Even though Bob is the smartest boy in his class, he is humble.

English: iron Pronunciation: İER-ro Spanish meaning: El hierro es un metal fuerte usado para muchos objetos. Meaning in English: Iron is a strong metal that is used to make many objects. Spanish example: El caballo tenía herraduras de hierro. Example in English: The horse had shoes made of iron. English source: Iron is a strong metal that is used to make many objects. The horse had shoes made of iron.

English: ladder Pronunciation: es-ka-LE-ra Spanish meaning: Una escalera se usa para subir y bajar. Meaning in English: A ladder is an object that is used to climb up and down things. Spanish example: Usó una escalera para subir al fuerte de su casa en el árbol. Example in English: He used a ladder to climb to the top of his tree house. English source: A ladder is an object that is used to climb up and down things. He used a ladder to climb to the top of his tree house.

English: modest Pronunciation: mo-DES-to Spanish meaning: Una persona modesta no se cree muy importante. Meaning in English: If people are modest, they do not think that they are too important. Spanish example: Derek es muy modesto para alguien tan rico. Example in English: Derek is very modest for someone who is so rich. English source: If people are modest, they do not think that they are too important. Derek is very modest for someone who is so rich.

English: occupy Pronunciation: o-ku-PAR Spanish meaning: Ocupar un lugar es vivir, trabajar o estar allí. Meaning in English: To occupy a place is to live, work, or be there. English: penny Pronunciation: sen-TA-bo Spanish meaning: Un centavo es una moneda de un céntimo. Meaning in English: A penny is a coin worth one cent. Spanish example: El rostro de Abraham Lincoln está en el centavo. Example in English: US President Abraham Lincoln is on the penny. English source: A penny is a coin worth one cent. US President Abraham Lincoln is on the penny.

English: preach Pronunciation: pre-di-KAR Spanish meaning: Predicar es hablar sobre una idea religiosa. Meaning in English: To preach is to talk about and promote a religious idea. Grammar: Part of speech: verb Forms: infinitive: prosperar; -ar pattern: yo -o; tú -as; él/ella -a; nosotros -amos; ellos -an; check irregular or stem-changing forms separately English source: To prosper is to be successful or make a lot of money. Frank’s new business finally prospered after many years of hard work.

English: province Pronunciation: pro-BİN-sia Spanish meaning: Una provincia es un área controlada por un país. Meaning in English: A province is an area that is controlled by a country. Spanish example: Canadá se divide en varias provincias. Example in English: Canada is divided into several different provinces. English source: A province is an area that is controlled by a country. Canada is divided into several different provinces.

English: satisfaction Pronunciation: sa-tis-fa-KSİON Spanish meaning: La satisfacción es el sentimiento de haber hecho o recibido algo bueno. Meaning in English: Satisfaction is the feeling of having done or received something good. Spanish example: Brad se llenó de satisfacción al ver qué había para cenar. Example in English: Brad was filled with satisfaction when he saw what was for dinner. English source: Satisfaction is the feeling of having done or received something good. Brad was filled with satisfaction when he saw what was for dinner.

English: sustain Pronunciation: man-te-NER Spanish meaning: Mantener algo es mantenerlo en marcha. Meaning in English: To sustain something is to keep it going. Spanish example: La energía eólica permite sostener una ciudad con energía. Example in English: Wind power is a clean way to sustain a city with energy. English: acquire Pronunciation: ad-ki-RİR Spanish meaning: Adquirir algo es obtener la posesión de eso. Meaning in English: To acquire something is to get or gain possession of that thing. Tina acquired a strange package yesterday.

English: awkward Pronunciation: in-KO-mo-do Spanish meaning: Algo incómodo es embarazoso y pone en una situación difícil. Meaning in English: An awkward thing is embarrassing and uncomfortable. Spanish example: Tras dejar caer su taza de café, Robbie se sintió incómodo. Example in English: After dropping his coffee cup, Robbie felt awkward. English source: An awkward thing is embarrassing and uncomfortable. After dropping his coffee cup, Robbie felt awkward.

English: caretaker Pronunciation: kui-da-DOR Spanish meaning: Un cuidador atiende a personas muy jóvenes, viejas o enfermas. Meaning in English: A caretaker is a person who takes care of very young, old, or sick people. Spanish example: El cuidador de mi abuela la ayuda a moverse por la casa. Example in English: My grandmother’s caretaker helps her get around the house. English source: A caretaker is a person who takes care of very young, old, or sick people. My grandmother’s caretaker helps her get around the house.

English: deceive Pronunciation: en-ga-NYAR Spanish meaning: Engañar es hacer que alguien crea algo falso. Meaning in English: To deceive is to make someone believe something that is not true. Mr. Perry discouraged the students from quitting school.

English: fake Pronunciation: FAL-so Spanish meaning: Algo falso está hecho para parecer real y engañar. Meaning in English: A fake thing is made to look real in order to trick people. Spanish example: El modelo llevaba un pelo falso. Example in English: The model was wearing fake hair. English source: A fake thing is made to look real in order to trick people. The model was wearing fake hair.

English: hatred Pronunciation: O-dio Spanish meaning: El odio es un fuerte sentimiento de no gustar a alguien o algo. Meaning in English: Hatred is a strong feeling of not liking someone or something. Spanish example: Tengo odio al sabor de la medicina. Example in English: I have a hatred for the taste of medicine. English source: Hatred is a strong feeling of not liking someone or something. I have a hatred for the taste of medicine.

English: hut Pronunciation: ÇO-sa Spanish meaning: Una choza es una casa pequeña de una o dos habitaciones. Meaning in English: A hut is a house made of wood, grass, or mud that has only one or two rooms. Spanish example: Entramos a la choza para dormir. Example in English: We all went into the hut to sleep. English source: A hut is a house made of wood, grass, or mud that has only one or two rooms. We all went into the hut to sleep.

English: inferior Pronunciation: in-fe-RİOR Spanish meaning: Un objeto inferior no es tan bueno como otro. Meaning in English: An inferior thing is not as good as something else. Spanish example: Los coches de hace cien años son inferiores a los actuales. Example in English: Cars built a hundred years ago are inferior to ones built today. English source: An inferior thing is not as good as something else. Cars built a hundred years ago are inferior to ones built today.

English: lodge Pronunciation: ka-BA-nya Spanish meaning: Una cabaña es una casa de montaña para cazadores o pescadores. English: neglect Pronunciation: ne-gli-HİR Spanish meaning: Descuidar a alguien o algo es no cuidarlo bien. Meaning in English: To neglect someone or something is to not take care of it properly. William neglected his room, so it is a complete mess.

English: newcomer Pronunciation: re-SİEN ye-GA-do Spanish meaning: Un recién llegado es alguien que llega recientemente. Meaning in English: A newcomer is a person who has recently arrived at a place or a group. Spanish example: Los estudiantes recibieron con alegría al recién llegado al colegio. English: offense Pronunciation: o-FEN-sa Spanish meaning: Una ofensa es una conducta indebida o delictiva. Meaning in English: An offense is behavior that is wrong or breaks a law. Spanish example: Robar un coche es una ofensa muy grave. Example in English: Stealing a car is a very serious offense. English source: An offense is behavior that is wrong or breaks a law. Stealing a car is a very serious offense.

column1: pasar por alto pa-SAR POR AL-to
column2: English: overlook Pronunciation: pa-SAR POR AL-to Spanish meaning: Pasar por alto algo es no darse cuenta de su importancia. Meaning in English: To overlook something is to not notice it or to not realize that it is important. Spanish example: Brenda pasó por alto el último paso y tuvo una mala caída. Example in English: Brenda overlooked the last step and had a bad fall. Grammar: Part of speech: verb Forms: infinitive: pasar; -ar pattern: yo -o; tú -as; él/ella -a; nosotros -amos; ellos -an; check irregular or stem-changing forms separately English source: To overlook something is to not notice it or to not realize that it is important. Brenda overlooked the last step and had a bad fall.

English: repay Pronunciation: reem-bol-SAR Spanish meaning: Reembolsar es pagar de vuelta o devolver algo. Meaning in English: To repay is to pay back or to reward someone or something. Grammar: Part of speech: verb Forms: infinitive: reembolsar; -ar pattern: yo -o; tú -as; él/ella -a; nosotros -amos; ellos -an; check irregular or stem-changing forms separately English source: To repay is to pay back or to reward someone or something. English: ridiculous Pronunciation: ri-Dİ-ku-lo Spanish meaning: Ridículo significa tonto o extraño. Meaning in English: A ridiculous thing is silly or strange. Spanish example: Steve se veía ridículo con esas gafas azules enormes. Example in English: Steve looked ridiculous with those huge blue sunglasses. English source: A ridiculous thing is silly or strange. Steve looked ridiculous with those huge blue sunglasses.

English: satisfactory Pronunciation: sa-tis-fa-KTO-rio Spanish meaning: Algo satisfactorio es lo suficientemente bueno. Meaning in English: A satisfactory thing is good enough. Spanish example: Mina sacaba calificaciones satisfactorias. Example in English: Mina often received satisfactory grades since she studied so hard. English source: A satisfactory thing is good enough. Mina often received satisfactory grades since she studied so hard.

English: shepherd Pronunciation: pas-TOR Spanish meaning: Un pastor protege y cuida a las ovejas. Meaning in English: A shepherd is a person who protects and cares for sheep. The shepherd moved the sheep to another field.

English: venture Pronunciation: a-ben-tu-RAR-se Spanish meaning: Aventurarse es ir a un lugar que puede ser peligroso. Meaning in English: To venture is to go to a place that may be dangerous. Spanish example: Aunque era peligroso, se aventuraron por la montaña. Example in English: Even though it was dangerous, they ventured up the mountain. English source: To venture is to go to a place that may be dangerous. Even though it was dangerous, they ventured up the mountain.

English: wheat Pronunciation: TRİ-go Spanish meaning: El trigo es una planta de la que se obtiene grano para hacer pan. The alley behind my house looks dirty.

English: ax Pronunciation: A-ça Spanish meaning: Un hacha es una herramienta para cortar madera. Meaning in English: An ax is a tool used to cut wood. Spanish example: Usó un hacha para cortar leña para la chimenea. Example in English: She used an ax to cut some wood for the fire. English source: An ax is a tool used to cut wood. She used an ax to cut some wood for the fire.

English: bunch Pronunciation: ra-Sİ-mo Spanish meaning: Un racimo es un grupo de cosas iguales. Meaning in English: A bunch is a group of the same things. English: outweigh Pronunciation: pre-ba-le-SER Spanish meaning: Prevalecer significa ser más importante o tener más peso que otra necesidad. Meaning in English: To outweigh something means to exceed it in value, amount, or importance. Spanish example: Encontrar un lugar cálido para dormir fue más importante que encontrar algo para comer. Example in English: Finding a warm place to sleep outweighed the need to find something to eat. English: paradox Pronunciation: pa-ra-DO-ha Spanish meaning: Una paradoja es una afirmación o hecho verdadero que parece ilógico. Meaning in English: A paradox is a true statement or real event that seems illogical. Spanish example: La paradoja de su trabajo era que cuanto menos trabajaba, más terminaba. Example in English: The paradox of her work was that the less she worked, the more she got done. English source: A paradox is a true statement or real event that seems illogical. English: tariff Pronunciation: a-ran-SEL Spanish meaning: Un arancel es un impuesto o tarifa sobre ciertas importaciones o exportaciones. Meaning in English: A tariff is a tax or fee paid on certain imports or exports. Spanish example: Nuestro arancel sobre la lana importada encarece la lana extranjera. Example in English: Our tariff on imported wool makes foreign wool more expensive. English source: A tariff is a tax or fee paid on certain imports or exports. English: thermometer Pronunciation: ter-MO-me-tro Spanish meaning: El termómetro es un aparato que mide la temperatura. Meaning in English: A thermometer is a device that measures temperature. English: vice versa Pronunciation: bi-se-BER-sa Spanish meaning: “Viceversa” significa invertir el orden de las dos ideas principales. Meaning in English: If a statement is vice versa, then its two main ideas are switched with one another. Spanish example: Los estudiantes aprenden de sus profesores, y viceversa. Example in English: Students learn from their teachers, and vice versa. English source: If a statement is vice versa, then its two main ideas are switched with one another. English: whereabouts Pronunciation: pa-ra-DE-ro Spanish meaning: El paradero de alguien o algo es el lugar donde está.
